exemplar.title: An Investigation into the Theory of Reconstructive Memory

detailedExemplarData.title: An Investigation into the Theory of Reconstructive Memory

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear, explicit aim with relevance explained in context of teenage media exposure
Thorough description of reconstructive memory theory and explicit linkage to the investigation
Precise operationalisation of IV and DV with fully articulated, testable hypotheses
weaknesses: Minor unfinished transition in theory-model description mid-sentence
Over-reliance on a secondary source (Iresearchnet, 2022) rather than primary empirical studies
Relevance explanation could be deepened by discussing ethical implications of working with minors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction excels in stating an explicit aim, elucidating its relevance, and grounding the study in reconstructive memory theory. Hypotheses and variables are fully operationalized and testable. Minor lapses in source balance and a truncated description of schema activation preclude the top mark, so a score of 5 is justified.

strengths: Well-explained choice of independent-measures design demonstrating control of demand characteristics
Comprehensive description and justification of convenience sampling and participant characteristics
Controlled variables and materials are not only listed but their selection rationale is clearly explained
weaknesses: Justification for video clip selection could be expanded to address ecological validity
Materials list would benefit from an explicit link between each material choice and the research aim
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration section fully meets the âexplainedâ requirements across research design, sampling, and variables/materials. It goes beyond description with clear rationale in each strand, warranting the maximum 4.

strengths: Accurate application of both descriptive statistics and an appropriate inferential test (Mann-Whitney U) with correct p-value interpretation
Graphical presentation is technically correct, addresses the hypothesis, and clearly labels axes and error bars
Findings are interpreted and linked back to the hypothesis, with correct rejection of the null
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in interpretation language regarding null hypothesis retention vs. rejection
Graph would benefit from showing a zero origin on the y-axis for full clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis demonstrates appropriately chosen and accurately executed statistical techniques, an effective graphical presentation, and a clear link between results and hypothesis. Despite small formatting and language inconsistencies, the top band descriptor is satisfied, meriting a 6.

strengths: Discussion of findings explicitly references reconstructive memory theory and the Loftus & Palmer model
Strengths and limitations are clearly explained and directly tied to validity and hypothesis testing
Proposed modifications are explicitly linked to identified limitations and justified
weaknesses: Depth of discussion could be increased by exploring ecological validity or alternative theoretical interpretations
Some modification rationales could more strongly emphasize their connection to specific limitations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation is consistently strong: theoretical discussion, clear articulation of strengths/limitations, and justified modifications. Slight brevity in theoretical depth and modification linkage prevents a perfect 6, so a score of 5 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar graph with error bars (Â± SD) is presented, correctly labels groups and axes in km/h, and directly addresses the hypothesis.  While aesthetically basic, it is technically correct and free of major errors, satisfying the highest descriptor.  Small formatting issues warrant a 5 rather than 6.

The student summarises statistical outcomes, correctly states that the null is rejected, and links findings to the pattern of means and standard deviations.  Interpretation explicitly connects back to the research hypothesis.  Some contradictory wording about ânull retainedâ in an earlier sentence shows slight inconsistency, so a 5 is awarded.

Both IV (verb intensity: âsmashedâ vs âcontactedâ) and DV (estimated speed in km/h) are correctly stated and **fully operationalised** (specific verbs; quantitative km/h scale).  Separate null and research hypotheses are written in precise, testable form.  The strand fully satisfies the 5-6 descriptor and shows no shortcomings, warranting the top mark in the band.

Convenience sampling is identified and its choice justified in practical terms.  Participant characteristics (gender split, age range, language background, absence of driving experience, no prior psychology study) are explained with reference to control of extraneous variables, thus fulfilling the âexplainedâ requirement of band 3-4.  Detail merits the top of the band.

Reconstructive memory and schemas are accurately described with textbook definitions and supporting citations.  The student **explicitly links** schema activation through leading verbs to the expected memory distortion in the present investigation.  Because the linkage is clear and conceptually accurate, the work fits the top band; slight brevity in contrasting theories justifies a 5 not 6.

Results are **discussed** (not merely described) with explicit reference to reconstructive memory, schemas, and the Loftus & Palmer findings, thereby situating the study within theoretical context.  The discussion is coherent but could delve deeper into alternative explanations; hence 5 within the top band.

The student clearly states an explicit aim: to study the effect of verb emotional intensity on teenage speed estimates.  Relevance is not merely identified but explicitly **explained** â teenagers are beginning to engage with media and may be vulnerable to language-based memory distortion.  This meets the descriptor for band 5-6; minor depth limitations (e.g., no societal implication data) keep the strand at 5 rather than 6.

Multiple strengths and limitations are **explained** and directly tied to design, sampling, and procedure, all relevant to validity and hypothesis testing.  Arguments show insight but are not exhaustively comprehensive, earning a solid 5.

Key controlled variables (same video, timing, questionnaire duration) and materials (video, questionnaires, consent/debrief forms) are listed **and reasons for their control/selection are provided**.  This meets the criterion for âexplainedâ (band 3-4).  Comprehensive but not exceptionally sophisticated; hence 4.

Each identified limitation is followed by a concrete, justified modification (lunchtime testing, older participants with driving experience, specifying measurement units).  The link between limitation and fix is explicit and rationale is provided, satisfying the highest descriptor; slight brevity keeps the award at 5, not 6.

The student names an independent-measures experiment and **explains** why this design was chosen over a repeated-measures alternative (demand characteristics).  Explanation goes beyond a mere description, aligning with the 3-4 band.  The account is clear and accurate, so the top of this band (4) is appropriate.

Means, standard deviations, and an appropriate non-parametric Mann-Whitney U test for two independent groups with small sample sizes are correctly calculated.  P-value interpretation is accurate (p = 0.046 < 0.05).  Minor transparency gaps in assumption checking prevent a perfect 6, but application is accurate, placing it solidly at 5 within the top band.

annotations.comment: The explanation of schema function in memory reconstruction shows depth of theoretical understanding and establishes relevance to the present investigation.
The transition to the Loftus and Palmer study is cut off mid-sentence; ensure the description of how leading verbs activate schemas and distort recall is completed for clarity.
The title succinctly identifies the study focus on reconstructive memory. Consider specifying variables like âverb intensityâ and participant demographics to clarify the investigation scope.
The title is descriptive but could be more focused by indicating the participant group and measurement; e.g., âEffect of Verb Intensity on Teen Speed Estimates.â
The explanation of reconstructive memory theory via Iresearchnet (2022) provides a clear definition, but reliance on secondary sources could be balanced by citing primary empirical studies.
The demographic strength highlighting gender balance supports population validity effectively.
Procedural modification suggestions are relevant but would benefit from explicit linkage to the earlier identified limitations for stronger justification.
Scheduling modification to lunchtime demonstrates ethical consideration and effectively addresses class interruption.
Highlighting unit specification enhances data reliability and addresses a practical procedural flaw.
The conclusion reiterates the p-value result; expand this section to discuss broader theoretical implications or suggest directions for future research.
The description of the original Loftus and Palmer (1974) methodology is concise and accurately situates this investigation within established research.
The relevance explanation links teen media exposure to memory distortion effectively; consider expanding on ethical implications of working with minors to deepen the rationale.
Independent variable and its operationalisation are clearly articulated, specifying âsmashedâ vs âcontactedâ verbs with precision.
The research hypothesis is precise, testable, and clearly links the independent and dependent variables.
The null hypothesis is correctly stated; refine wording to specify acceptance criterion (e.g., pâ¥0.05) for alignment with the inferential procedure.
The rationale for choosing an independent measures design over repeated measures is well explained in terms of demand characteristic concerns.
Sampling method and participant characteristics are comprehensively described and justified with respect to control of extraneous variables.
Controlled variables are specified with reasons for control, demonstrating careful attention to internal validity.
Materials list is thorough; include a brief justification for the video clip selection in terms of realism and ecological validity.
Descriptive statistics table is clearly formatted, presenting means and standard deviations for both conditions.
Interpretation suggests retaining the null based on overlap, yet the inferential test later rejects it; ensure consistency in findings interpretation.
The bar graph addresses the hypothesis but the y-axis origin isnât shown; explicitly marking zero would improve interpretability.
The p-value interpretation as â95.4% sureâ conflates confidence with p; revise to reflect the correct probability interpretation (p<0.05 indicates significance).
The discussion aptly ties results back to reconstructive memory theory and schemas, reinforcing theoretical integration.
Consider discussing ecological validity and how classroom testing conditions may limit generalisability beyond the lab setting.
